translations.en: O slaves of Allah! The day of resurrection is more severe than the grave. A day when the young will grow old, the old ones get inebriated; wombs will abort, every suckling mother distracted from her child; a dark dreadful day whose evil will be rampant. The angels who have no sins will be scared by the terror of that day, the hard seven skies will tremble, together with the peg-like mountains and the couch-like earth. The heavens will be rent asunder, tottering, turning red like pigment. Mountains shall turn into heaps of sand. The trumpet will be blown, and those in the heavens and earth will be terrified except whoever Allah wills. So what of those who sinned with their ears, eyes, tongue, hand, foot, private parts and stomach – if Allah does not forgive them? For they will be judged and consigned to fire whose pit is deep, heat severe, drink is stinking water, chastisement ever renewed with iron rods. They will not be relieved of its torment nor will the inmates die. An abode where there is no mercy and prayers of the inmates will not be heard.
